cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which description about Yves Gomes is true based on the given passage. First, let me go through each option carefully and compare them with the information in the text. Option A: He was born in India before his parents immigrated to America illegally. Hmm. The passage says he left India with his parents when he was 14 months old. That means he was born in India? Wait, the passage says he's one of the young illegal immigrants who grew up in the US. So he came to the US when he was 14 months old, implying he was born in India. But his parents were deported more than a year ago. Wait, but he's now in the US as an illegal immigrant. So if he was born in India and came to the US when he was 14 months old, then he was brought in illegally. So his parents were the ones who immigrated illegally. So option A says he was born in India before his parents immigrated to America illegally. Wait, no. The parents were already in the US when he was 14 months old? Or he was born in India, then his parents took him to the US when he was 14 months old. So the parents were the ones who immigrated illegally. The passage says Yves was brought to the US as a young child. So he was born in India, and his family came to the US illegally when he was 14 months old. So option A says he was born in India before his parents immigrated to America illegally. Wait, that would mean his parents first immigrated illegally, then he was born in India? That doesn't fit.
